meta.description: The Fluttering Sail — LLM Provider Configuration
meta.note: This file controls which LLM provider is used for ingestion (agent_expand.py) and synthesis (app.py). All providers must expose an OpenAI-compatible API. Most major providers do: OpenAI natively, Anthropic via compatibility layer, Google via OpenRouter, local models via Ollama. To use a non-OpenAI-compatible provider natively (e.g. direct Anthropic SDK, direct Google SDK), a provider adapter would be required — see Future Work in the paper.
meta._note_openai_compatible: OpenAI-compatible means the provider accepts POST requests to /v1/chat/completions with the same JSON body structure as OpenAI and returns responses in the same format. This is now an industry standard. OpenRouter (openrouter.ai) proxies 100+ models behind this interface and is the recommended approach for switching models without code changes.

_note_provider: Identifier for the provider — used only for logging. Does not affect API calls. Examples: openai, anthropic-via-openrouter, gemini-via-openrouter, groq, ollama-local
_note_api_key_env_var: Name of the environment variable containing the API key. The application reads os.environ[api_key_env_var]. Common values: OPENAI_API_KEY (OpenAI), OPENROUTER_API_KEY (OpenRouter — works for Claude/Gemini/Llama), GROQ_API_KEY (Groq), ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (Anthropic native — requires adapter). Set with: export YOUR_KEY_NAME=sk-...
_note_base_url: API base URL. Change this to switch providers without touching Python. Examples: https://api.openai.com/v1 (OpenAI default), https://openrouter.ai/api/v1 (OpenRouter — access Claude/Gemini/Llama with same code), http://localhost:11434/v1 (Ollama local), https://api.groq.com/openai/v1 (Groq). The /v1/chat/completions path is appended automatically.
_note_timeout_seconds: Request timeout in seconds. Increase for slower local models or large batches. Default 60 is adequate for GPT-4o and most cloud providers.
